description_long: This sculpture depicts a mythological faun or satyr, seated on a tree stump and playing a flute. The figure has the upper body of a muscular man with wild, curly hair and pointed ears, while the lower body is covered in fur and ends in goat-like legs and hooves. The faun's posture is relaxed yet expressive, with one leg crossed over the other and his head bent down as he focuses on his instrument. The tree stump base is textured with bark details and naturalistic irregularities, grounding the figure in a woodland setting. The surface of the sculpture has a bronze-like patina, giving it an aged, classical appearance. The overall craftsmanship is detailed, with careful attention to anatomy, fur, and the organic forms of both the faun and the stump.
description: A detailed bronze-like sculpture of a faun or satyr seated on a tree stump, playing a flute, with realistic anatomy and textured fur and bark.
description_view_0: Front view showing the faun's expressive face, flute, and crossed legs, with detailed fur and muscular torso visible.
description_view_1: Rear three-quarter view highlighting the faun's back, wild hair, and the textured tree stump, with the flute partially obscured.
description_view_2: Direct rear view focusing on the faun's back muscles, fur, and the full width of the tree stump base.
description_view_3: Side view emphasizing the faun's bent head, arm holding the flute, and the curvature of the seated posture.
attribute_subject_matter: mythological faun or satyr playing a flute
attribute_location_context: No visible context; background is isolated and does not indicate a specific setting.
